channel.title: The New Yorker — Reporting
channel.description: Latest reporting from The New Yorker

item.title: How the Internet Fringe Infiltrated Republican Politics
item.description: Inside the battle for the post-MAGAG.O.P.

item.title: Radu Jude, the Bard of Bucharest
item.description: The director’s native city drives him crazy—and drives him to make loony, brilliant films.

item.title: The Car-Crash Conspiracy
item.description: High-speed accidents, crooked lawyers, and poor people desperate for cash—it was the kind of scheme that could have been cooked up only in the Big Easy.

item.title: Why I Wanted to Keep My Marriage a Secret
item.description: It’s not that I was embarrassed by Hugh or that I thought someone better might come along. I just shudder when I hear a man say the words “my husband.”

item.title: The Return of Family Detention
item.description: Under the Trump Administration, thousands of immigrant children have been detained, and many have suffered from medical neglect.

item.title: El retorno de la detención familiar
item.description: Durante el gobierno de Trump, miles de niños inmigrantes han sido detenidos y muchos han sufrido de negligencia médica.

item.title: When Your Digital Life Vanishes
item.description: A broken phone or corrupted drive can mean the loss of work, evidence, art, or the last traces of the dead. But sometimes data-recovery experts can summon lost files from the void.

item.title: The Action-Film Director Who’s Taking On Michael Jackson
item.description: Antoine Fuqua has built a career on movies with irresistible heroes. Now he’s telling the story of the King of Pop.

item.title: Tabula Rasa: Volume Six
item.description: A project meant not to end.

item.title: How Professional Wrestling Prepared Linda McMahon for Trump’s Cabinet
item.description: The Education Secretary ran the W.W.E. for years with her husband, Vince, an unstable man who, like her new boss, has a genius for inflaming the crowd.
